paper_title: More is not always better: Nonlinear effects of window-to-wall ratio and visual complexity on spatial perception in indoor sports spaces

questionnaire_significance.method_policy: Use item-level LMM and WWR trend contrasts as diagnostics; do not replace primary multimodal models or force a global scale.

claim_policy.wwr_nonlinearity: Three WWR levels support trend/planned-contrast language only, not a strong optimality claim.
claim_policy.questionnaire_composites: Afford4/Bmean/IPQ_mean are supplementary unless reliability, missingness, and construct rationale are documented.
claim_policy.ipq_interpretation: IPQ is participant-level and should not be interpreted as scene-level WWR or Complexity evidence.
claim_policy.eeg_interpretation: EEG cognitive-processing claims require convergent questionnaire or eye-tracking evidence.
claim_policy.experience_moderation: Experience claims require balanced groups and sensitivity checks.
